title: myrtus communis subsp. baetica
intro: Myrtus communis subsp. baetica, also known as Spanish myrtle, is a subspecies of the myrtle plant native to southern Europe and North Africa. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin infections, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate stress. Myrtus communis subsp. baetica can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
